key_risks: Hot June CPI/PPI prints (released after CPI window) or hawkish FOMC tone on June 17 could reverse the rate-driven growth rally
Semiconductor and Taiwan concentration creates geopolitical and single-theme drawdown risk if AI/chip sentiment cools
Headline CPI YoY re-accelerating to 4.2% and rising energy CPI could pressure multiples and the rate-cut narrative
Regional banks remain sensitive to any credit or deposit shock that could trigger sharp idiosyncratic drawdowns

portfolio.rationale: Strongest momentum across all timeframes (8.8% 7d, 75% 6m, 136% 1y) with AI capex tailwind and falling yields supporting growth multiples.
Best-in-class factor momentum (6.2% 30d) capturing the leadership trend; lower single-sector risk than pure semis.
Semiconductor supply-chain leverage with broad strength (8.2% 30d, 67% 6m), benefiting from global electronics demand.
Strong recent momentum (9.3% 30d) as falling 2Y/10Y yields steepen curve modestly and credit conditions stay benign; cyclical catch-up trade.
ISM manufacturing expansion (54.0) and new orders rising plus durable/factory orders surge support cyclical industrials into a soft-landing window.

portfolio_rationale: Cooling core inflation, falling Treasury yields, low VIX, and a near-certain FOMC hold favor risk-on growth and cyclical momentum; tilt toward semiconductors/AI leadership while diversifying into rate-sensitive cyclicals.
rationale_summary: Lean into the dominant momentum/semiconductor leadership trend supported by disinflation in core CPI and falling yields, balanced with cyclical financials and industrials showing accelerating data.
